post.topic: Claude API Performance Optimization Techniques for Production Apps
post.hook: 4 proven ways to optimize Claude API performance and reduce costs in production 🚀
post.caption:
4 proven ways to optimize Claude API performance and reduce costs in production 🚀

Save this carousel for your next Claude API project.

slides.title: Use Async for Batch Requests
slides.body: Process multiple Claude requests concurrently instead of sequentially. This reduces total response time by up to 70%.
slides.code_snippet:
import asyncio
import anthropic

async def batch_process(prompts):
    tasks = [client.messages.create(model="claude-3-haiku-20240307", messages=[{"role": "user", "content": p}]) for p in prompts]
    return await asyncio.gather(*tasks)

slides.title: Cache Responses with Redis
slides.body: Store Claude responses in Redis to avoid redundant API calls. Perfect for FAQ bots and repeated queries.
slides.code_snippet:
import redis
r = redis.Redis()

response = r.get(prompt_hash)
if not response:
    response = client.messages.create(...)
    r.setex(prompt_hash, 3600, response.content[0].text)

slides.title: Choose Right Model Tier
slides.body: Use Haiku for simple tasks, Sonnet for complex reasoning. Haiku is 5x faster and costs 90% less than Opus.
slides.code_snippet:
# Simple classification - use Haiku
model = "claude-3-haiku-20240307"

# Complex analysis - use Sonnet
model = "claude-3-5-sonnet-20241022"

slides.title: Implement Smart Token Limits
slides.body: Set max_tokens based on your use case. Shorter limits reduce latency and costs significantly.
slides.code_snippet:
response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-haiku-20240307",
    max_tokens=150,  # Adjust based on need
    messages=[{"role": "user", "content": prompt}]
)
